title: Nanny From Hell
theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.
omen: an anniversary card mailed after the funeral
relationship_dynamic: perfect new husband with archived widow energy

starter_premise.title: Nanny From Hell
starter_premise.premise: She has a whole fake family planned, complete with albums, a ring, and a baby name.
starter_premise.signature_tell: There's a shoebox and nobody wants to see inside it.
starter_premise.base_premise: Great with kids, wants to be the mother, and intends to become the mother.

beat_sheet.text: From scene one the audience sees enough to dread the house, the husband, or the helper correctly before Brooke Bell does. She has a whole fake family planned, complete with albums, a ring, and a baby name. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Comfort and threat are fused immediately, so the category never pretends normalcy is truly available. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.09. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

A domestic ritual involving snow globe turns uncanny early enough to make the rest of the movie feel trapped in aftermath. Signature tell: There's a shoebox and nobody wants to see inside it. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.19.

Claire Mercer names the danger almost plainly, then retreats, leaving Brooke Bell with nobody credible enough to trust. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.30. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

Act one ends with dread confirmed for us and denied to her. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.41.

The authority beat turns hostile: police, therapist, doctor, or partner actively help trap Brooke Bell inside the wrong story. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.54.

The ally is compromised or removed before act three, ensuring the climax belongs to isolation rather than teamwork. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Brooke Bell hesitates before explaining dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.68. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

Extreme-only beat: Brooke Bell finds the shoebox, second family, clinic paperwork, or fake archive proving this horror has serial architecture. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Brooke Bell hesitates before explaining dock office with a desk nobody fully claims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.83.
The house or system now behaves like a closed loop designed to recycle victims. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.99. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.
By the end of act two escape requires disobedience, not explanation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.17.

The confrontation turns survival into an act of contamination management rather than clean heroism. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.37.

Brooke Bell gets out only partially intact, and the category refuses to pretend that exposure erases residue. Elias Bell hesitates before explaining old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

A symbolic act involving boat key secures survival at the cost of permanent psychic theft. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.81. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

The ending leaves the dread alive in memory, paperwork, or the children, even if the villain is finished. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Claire Mercer hesitates before explaining church basement described as just storage. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 3.07. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Sienna Pruitt is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Brooke Bell says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Winter Hollow.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Sienna?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Luke. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Brooke. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The snow globe is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Claire."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "Nanny From Hell understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "Nanny From Hell" follows Brooke Bell, a recovery protagonist with paperwork whose life seemed manageable at first, even after perfect new husband with archived widow energy opened a new room inside it. Sienna Pruitt seemed helpful. The house seemed calm. Even an anniversary card mailed after the funeral could have seemed incidental if anyone were still interested in the difference between comfort and staging. The governing injury is simple: She has a whole fake family planned, complete with albums, a ring, and a baby name. The signature tell is always this: There's a shoebox and nobody wants to see inside it. Everything seemed fine until it very plainly is not, and the narrator keeps talking softly enough to make that failure feel deliberate. Inspired by something, probably.
